summary:
Based on extensive research indicating their high frequency as initial immune cells at infected areas along with robust mechanisms involving receptor detection leading up to internal destruction processes, it can be concluded that bacteria are predominantly ENGULFed by NEUTROPHILs rather than other types such as LGLs due primarily to speed and efficiency factors associated specifically with them compared against larger counterparts or less frequent contributors generally speaking across various contexts studied thus far regarding bacterial defense strategies employed throughout human physiology under threat conditions posed directly from microbial invasion events themselves.
Bacteria are typically engulfed by neutrophils because they respond quickly to infections and have effective mechanisms for recognizing and destroying invaders; no significant alternative agents were found to match both efficacy and prevalence consistently observed clinically when comparing different cell types involved in innate immunity responses towards microorganisms including but not limited to E.coli Staphylococcus aureus etc..
